markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Implement pins, favorites, unlinked blocks, and sorted views with backend state. This MT belongs to LoomObsidianNavigation. Deliver the Obsidian replacement surface as a Handshake-native Loom projection.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Obsidian-like behavior must be implemented as Loom/project knowledge projections over PostgreSQL, not a markdown-vault clone.
Backlinks, graph, tags, folders, pins, color labels, and project links must have backend navigation APIs and visual-debug evidence.
scope.expected_behavior: PinsFavoritesAndUnlinked leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: PinsFavoritesAndUnlinked is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence.summary: Reorderable Pins grid + Unlinked triage backend for the four Loom views. The All/Unlinked/Sorted/Pins view queries were already built; MT-183 adds user-controlled pin ordering (the reorderable grid, [LM-VIEW-004]) and deterministic ordering.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - map_loom_block reads pin_order tolerantly (try_get) so the ~26 existing block SELECTs are unaffected; Pins view ORDER BY pin_order ASC NULLS LAST, updated_at DESC, block_id ASC; All/Unlinked get block_id tiebreaker; set_loom_block_pin_order (sets OR clears to NULL); update_loom_block patches pin_order; get_loom_block returns it

evidence.command: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils --test loom_pins_views_tests  &&  --test storage_conformance  &&  --lib storage::tests:: -- --test-threads=1
evidence.result: pins/views ok. 4 passed; storage_conformance ok. 3 passed (incl postgres_loom_storage_conformance); storage::tests ok. 25 passed; 0 failed (real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL)
tests: pins_view_respects_user_pin_order - pin_order C=0,A=1,B=2 -> order C,A,B; reorder B=-1 -> B,C,A; pin_order returned on single read
clearing_pin_order_sends_block_to_the_end - clear B (NULL) -> NULLS LAST trails ordered A
unlinked_view_excludes_linked_blocks - lonely block unlinked; mention-linked src/tgt excluded
REGRESSION: postgres_loom_storage_conformance (four-views) + 25 loom storage tests still green after the select_blocks ORDER BY change

evidence.summary: Recovered MT-183's missing Favorites surface: durable loom_blocks.favorite flag, Favorites view/API parser, favorite update tracking, favorite propagation through LoomBlock readers, strict mapper enforcement, and LoomViewQueried telemetry allowlist.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0296_loom_block_favorites.sql (NEW) - additive favorite flag plus partial favorites index
src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0296_loom_block_favorites.down.sql (NEW) - drops favorites index and favorite column
src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - writes favorite, reads favorite strictly, Favorites view filter, and all map_loom_block SQL projections include favorite
src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs - patch change tracking, /loom/views/favorites parsing, and Favorites result counting
src/backend/handshake_core/tests/loom_pins_views_tests.rs - real-Postgres Favorites and search propagation regressions
gaps_closed: Pins and Favorites are now distinct durable Loom surfaces.
Favorite-only blocks are not implicitly pinned.
Favorite flags survive non-Favorites read paths such as search results.
Favorites telemetry no longer fails recorder validation.

evidence.command: POSTGRES_TEST_URL=postgres://postgres@127.0.0.1:5565/handshake cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils --test loom_pins_views_tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture && cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils flight_recorder_loom_view_queried_accepts_favorites --lib -- --nocapture && POSTGRES_TEST_URL=postgres://postgres@127.0.0.1:5565/handshake cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils --test storage_conformance -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture && cargo check --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features runtime-full,test-utils && rustfmt --edition 2024 --check <targeted MT-183 files>
evidence.result: RED compile failure captured before implementation; after review fix, loom_pins_views_tests ok: 6 passed, 0 failed; flight_recorder favorites validator ok: 1 passed; storage_conformance ok: 3 passed; cargo check exit 0; targeted rustfmt --check exit 0.

evidence.summary: Russell initial review blocked MT-183 for incomplete favorite propagation and stale LoomViewQueried view_type validation. Follow-up review after fixes returned PASS and recommended READY_FOR_VALIDATION.
findings_closed: Every current map_loom_block SQL read path projects favorite and map_loom_block now fails loudly if favorite is omitted.
Flight recorder validation accepts the favorites view type.
API parser and telemetry use the same favorites literal.
create_loom_block coherently relies on the database default and returns favorite.

evidence.summary: KB ready checklist PASS emitted before READY_FOR_VALIDATION handoff.

findings: Initial FAIL: favorite was not propagated through every API-visible LoomBlock read path.
Initial FAIL: favorites view telemetry was rejected by the FlightRecorder validator.
Follow-up PASS: all prior findings closed by direct code changes and fresh proof.
fixes: All map_loom_block SQL read paths now project favorite; map_loom_block uses strict row.get("favorite").
Added real-Postgres search propagation regression and focused telemetry validator test.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-183 requires pins (reorderable grid), favorites, unlinked, and sorted Loom views backed by durable PostgreSQL state. The code implements all four as real PostgreSQL queries over loom_blocks/loom_edges with durable pin_order and favorite columns, real CRUD/API, and integration tests proven against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL (no SQLite/mock/Docker). It aligns with current Master Spec 7.1.4.3 / 11 [LM-VIEW-002][LM-VIEW-004].
findings: Four views are real PostgreSQL queries in query_loom_view (postgres.rs:6896-6945): Pins filters b.pinned != 0 ORDER BY b.pin_order ASC NULLS LAST, updated_at DESC, block_id ASC (reorderable grid per [LM-VIEW-004]); Favorites filters b.favorite != 0; Unlinked uses NOT EXISTS over loom_edges mention/tag edges; Sorted groups by edge. No parallel store.

Reorderable Pins grid is durable: migration 0293 adds nullable loom_blocks.pin_order + partial index; set_loom_block_pin_order (postgres.rs:10018) is a real UPDATE binding Option<i32> so NULL genuinely clears (not a COALESCE no-op), fails closed with NotFound on missing block; exposed via PUT /loom/blocks/:id/pin-order (api/loom.rs:112).

Favorites is a distinct durable surface: migration 0296 adds favorite INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 + partial index; map_loom_block reads favorite strictly via row.get('favorite') (postgres.rs:2019,2043) so any read path omitting it fails loudly; every map_loom_block SQL projection includes b.favorite; FlightRecorder LoomViewQueried allowlist includes 'favorites' (flight_recorder/mod.rs:2648); API parser maps 'favorites' literal (api/loom.rs:3113).

Unlinked semantics (NOT EXISTS where block is source OR target of a mention/tag edge) faithfully implement spec [LM-VIEW-002] 'zero MENTION + TAG edges'; verified by current Master Spec 07-user-experience-and-development.md:525-532 and 11-shared-dev-platform-and-oss-foundations.md:445-451.

Runtime proof is real: tests/loom_pins_views_tests.rs runs against real PostgreSQL via knowledge_pg_support.rs which resolves POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL/Handshake-managed ManagedPostgres with NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback (skips only when PG binaries genuinely absent, panics on any other failure). Tests cover pin ordering, reorder, NULL-clear, unlinked exclusion, fail-closed, favorites independence from pins, and favorite-flag survival through search. Evidence records 6 passed after review fix.

Sub-rule 1: grep of loom.rs/api loom.rs/test file found no todo!/unimplemented!/deferred/pending-MT/*Unavailable escapes on any live path; the single unreachable! (test:133) is a post-type-check assertion guard, not a live placeholder.
Sub-rule 3: lifecycle.claimed_by=KERNEL_BUILDER-20260614-175624, completed_by=null (status READY_FOR_VALIDATION) — no self-certified COMPLETED; an independent validator signs the transition.

All changes confined to allowed product paths (src/backend/handshake_core, migrations, tests); no /.GOV/, no SQLite, no Docker, no external search engine — packet forbidden list respected. Aside: external evidence logs under ../Handshake_Artifacts were not present on disk, which is expected and non-blocking.
evidence_checked: git show e856a5f9 (Pins commit) + c49afb7f (Favorites checkpoint)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
